reasoning: A human vaccine for Cryptococcosis is not currently approved, and there are currently no approved fungal vaccines in existence. A review of the scientific literature as of late 2025 confirms that the most advanced experimental vaccines for Cryptococcosis (such as mRNA LNP candidates) are still only being tested in murine (animal) pre-clinical models. Given the strict, multi-year clinical trial and regulatory approval process required for new vaccines, it is practically impossible for a candidate to progress from structural/animal studies to an approved vaccine in humans within the next month. Consequently, the Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' is highly unlikely to be updated to list a vaccine for Cryptococcosis prior to the late February or mid-March 2026 resolution dates. The only non-zero probability stems from random vandalism to the Wikipedia page that happens to be un-reverted on the exact resolution date, which is exceedingly low probability.
